# Meridian Osteopathy — AI-search audit

**Report date:** 2026-05-15
**Run cadence:** weekly (Saturday)
**Generated by:** weekly-routine

---

## TL;DR

- **Three items shipped this run (#28, #29, #30) — all via PR #71, which bundled PRs #68/#69/#70.** That's the second-largest single-run shipment since the audit began. #28 closed G14 (image alt text rewritten descriptive-not-titular across team profiles, condition pages, and service pages). #29 closed G22 (Hillmorton + Wigram + Addington + Halswell + Cashmere now appear in flowing intro copy on every service page in services.json, not just the footer line). #30 closed G23 (a 'Conditions we commonly treat' block on the osteopathy service page linking all six condition pages, plus a matching 'Common conditions we treat' section on the homepage). Two further non-punch-list improvements also shipped: PR #72 made /book/ AI-citable with a ReserveAction schema + visible intro content (relevant for AI-driven booking flows now that Perplexity Health launched 2026-03-19 with first-party EHR integration), and PR #73 added explicit www → apex 301 redirects in netlify.toml. PR #74 added Kaylee Frost as a sixth practitioner.
- **Search visibility — 31 queries re-checked:** Meridian still ranks 0 across every tracked query (sixth consecutive run with the same pattern). Every checked query lost 10 priority points per the decay rule. Three SERP movements worth tracking: 'dry needling halswell' recovered an NZ player (Elite Physio Halswell back at top 5 vs gone last week); 'osteopath addington' lost its only NZ player (Performance Plus disappeared), so the SERP is now fully UK-only — same hollow-SERP pattern as 'osteopath wigram'; 'osteopath hillmorton' is STILL all-UK results despite #29 shipping 2026-05-10 (Google likely hasn't re-indexed yet — calibration check is next run).
- **Industry signals — strongest finding:** May 2026 healthcare AEO/GEO research (ALM Corp, Conductor, BrightEdge, RankVed, Sapt) converges on a consistent opening pattern for AI-citation-eligible content: lead with a direct, concise answer to the query intent in the first 40-60 words, then expand. Our condition + service intros currently open with topic-framing rather than answer-leading copy — not bad prose, but not citation-optimised. That's a new gap (G24) and a new item (#31, Tier 2 / M / S). AI Overviews now trigger on 80%+ of healthcare queries; cited brands earn 35% more clicks and AI visitors convert 4.4× higher than organic. Perplexity Health launched 2026-03-19 with Apple Health + b.well EHR integration, making the "AI as healthcare front door" hypothesis less hypothetical.

---

## What changed since last run (2026-05-10)

- **Items shipped (3):**
  - **#28 — image alt text rewrite** (Tier 2 / M / S). PR #70, bundled into PR #71. `team-profile.njk` now uses `member.photoAlt` with name fallback; services.json, team.json, condition pages and service pages have descriptive alt strings ("Osteopath performing gentle cervical mobilisation…" pattern). Closes G14.
  - **#29 — in-content suburb signals** (Tier 2 / M / S). PR #68, bundled into PR #71. Every service intro in services.json now names Hillmorton + Halswell + Wigram + Addington + Cashmere in flowing copy: 'Our Hillmorton clinic serves patients across Halswell, Wigram, Addington, Cashmere and surrounding Christchurch suburbs.' Closes G22.
  - **#30 — cross-link condition pages** (Tier 2 / M / S). PR #69, bundled into PR #71. New 'Conditions we commonly treat' card on `/services/osteopathy/` linking all six condition pages; matching 'Common conditions we treat' button row on `/` (home). Closes G23.
- **Non-punch-list improvements (2):**
  - **PR #72 — /book/ page AI-citable:** added ReserveAction schema and visible intro content so ChatGPT / Perplexity / Gemini can recommend the page as a booking action, not just an interstitial.
  - **PR #73 — www → apex 301 redirects:** explicit redirect block in netlify.toml so Google consolidates link equity onto the canonical apex domain.
- **Team change:** PR #74 added Kaylee Frost as a sixth practitioner.
- **Items still pending (8 total):**
  - **Tier 1:** #17 (dry-needling sub-page)
  - **Tier 2:** #12 (speakable schema), #13 (auto-fetch GBP reviews), #25 (ACC page), **#31 (citation-ready opening pattern, NEW)**
  - **Tier 3:** #14 (blog), #15 (Reddit seeding), #16 (review nudge)
- **New punch-list item added this run:** #31 — Rewrite condition + service intros with a citation-ready 40–60 word direct answer first (addresses G24).
- **Pool changes:**
  - Top-31 priority queries re-checked against live SERPs.
  - 2 new derived queries added (autocomplete still blocked): `whiplash osteopath christchurch`, `concussion osteopath christchurch`. Both untested NZ ACC-adjacent conditions where competitor coverage is suspected thin.
  - Pool size 36 → 38 (well under 50 cap).
  - All 31 checked queries had `meridianRank: 0`, so all priorities decremented by 10 per the demote rule.

---

## Search visibility — wins, losses and movement vs 2026-05-08

### Wins
- None. Meridian is not in the top 10 for any tracked query (sixth straight run with this pattern).

### Notable SERP shifts (mixed signals)
- **dry needling halswell** — RECOVERED. Elite Physiotherapy Halswell is back at top 5 NZ-side this week (was completely gone last week, present 2 weeks ago). The suburb+modality SERP is now partially contested again. Reinforces (but doesn't change) item #17's argument that the dry-needling sub-page should name Halswell.
- **osteopath addington** — REGRESSED in the opposite direction: Performance Plus Physio Addington (sole NZ player last week) is gone this week, so the SERP is now fully UK-only. Same hollow-SERP pattern as 'osteopath wigram'. Item #29 just shipped Addington into flowing intro copy — re-check next run.
- **osteopath wigram** — STILL fully uncontested NZ-side. Health4You aggregator + UK osteopath sites + cancerresearchuk.org filling the SERP. #29 just added Wigram to intro copy; calibration check next run.
- **osteopath hillmorton** — STILL all UK results (Winchmore Hill / Hertfordshire / Boston / Halifax). Google has not yet picked up #29's addition of Hillmorton to intro copy. Re-check next run.

### Indexing-lag tracking (pages shipped 3-5 weeks ago)
| Page | Shipped | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/conditions/sciatica/` | ~5 weeks ago | not surfacing | #30 just added internal links — calibration check is next run |
| `/conditions/headaches-migraines/` | ~5 weeks ago | not surfacing | osteopath-christchurch.com still dominant via blog cadence |
| `/conditions/pregnancy-back-pain/` | ~5 weeks ago | not surfacing | Better Health and osteopathiccare.org own the SERP |
| `/conditions/sports-injury-recovery/` | ~5 weeks ago | not surfacing | Same competitor set |
| `/conditions/neck-pain/` | ~3 weeks ago | not surfacing | Better Health's /conditions/neck-pain/ ranks; ours doesn't |
| `/conditions/back-pain/` | ~3 weeks ago | not surfacing | Better Health has 2 URL variants ranking |
| `/services/acupuncture/fertility/` | ~3 weeks ago | not surfacing | nikauroom.co.nz (Nina Hu's other-address competitor) still in top 5 |

The pattern is now clear: shipping a page is necessary but not sufficient. #30 (internal cross-links) was the structural fix; the next 1-2 runs are the calibration window for whether internal-link surface alone closes the indexing-lag gap, or whether items #14 (blog freshness signals) and #31 (citation-ready openings) become the next levers.

### Competitor watch
- **Better Health Osteopathy** continues to dominate the most valuable SERPs (best osteopath christchurch, neck pain, back pain, sciatica, frozen shoulder, jaw pain, pregnancy back pain, baby osteopath, sports injury). Multiple URL variants ranking on several queries — they've doubled down on the /conditions/* pattern AND the homepage. This is what /conditions/* + #30 cross-links + #14 (blog) collectively try to defend against.
- **osteopath-christchurch.com (Biodynamic)** continues to win every cranial / niche / blog-cadence query (headaches, migraines, jaw pain, teeth grinding, cranial, baby, stress, arthritis). One short blog post per condition — same item #14 pattern.
- **osteo.co.nz** owns Halswell + ACC + sciatica clusters. Their dedicated /acc/ URL still wins 'osteopath acc christchurch'.
- **MK Osteopathy** still owns 'tmj osteopath christchurch' on the same blog-velocity pattern.

---

## Industry signals worth noting

| Source | Finding | Implication for Meridian |
|---|---|---|
| ALM Corp / RankVed (May 2026) | Citation-ready content opens with a direct concise answer in the first 40-60 words, then expands | **New gap G24, new item #31.** Our intros are topic-framing, not answer-leading |
| BrightEdge (May 2026) | AI Overviews now trigger on 80%+ of healthcare queries; clinical content at 93-100% coverage | Reinforces every existing AEO item. Local "near me" queries are an exception — AI Overviews have backed off there |
| Conductor / Profound (May 2026) | AI platforms scan for agreement across multiple independent sources before confidently citing a brand | Item #15 (Reddit seeding) + organic mentions corroborate our schema. Schema alone isn't enough |
| Sapt / Yext (May 2026) | Cited brands earn 35% more clicks; AI visitors convert 4.4× higher than organic | Citation isn't a vanity metric — it's higher-quality traffic |
| Perplexity Health launch (2026-03-19) | First-party healthcare AI search with Apple Health + b.well EHR integration | PR #72 (/book/ ReserveAction schema) shipped this week was directly motivated by this — AI assistants are increasingly handling the booking step |
| Contently / SE Ranking (April 2026) | Reddit is now the most-cited domain across all major AI models (450% growth in AI-overview citations YoY) | Item #15 still holds. Reddit is the single highest-leverage off-site surface for AI citation share |
| llms.txt 2026 audits | No major AI vendor has officially committed to reading llms.txt in production; current retrieval still relies on web search + schema.org + direct page fetches | Item #26 (alternate link tag) shipped last week was a low-cost hedge — it costs nothing and might pay off; schema.org is still the load-bearing layer |
| BrightEdge healthcare 2023-2025 retro | Local "near me" queries went from 100% AI Overview presence in 2023 to 0% today — Google has been deliberately backing off there | Reduces the urgency on "near me" optimisation; sharpens the case for content-led optimisation (blog, condition pages, citation-ready openings) over local-tag stuffing |

## Punch list (current state)

### Tier 1 (1 item)
- **#17 — /services/acupuncture/dry-needling/ sub-page (Western framing)** — keep main acupuncture page TCM-first. Mirror the existing /services/acupuncture/fertility/ pattern. Addresses G16.

### Tier 2 (4 items)
- **#12 — speakable schema on service intros** (voice / Gemini Live). Addresses G7.
- **#13 — auto-fetch GBP reviews at build time** (so review recency stays current).
- **#25 — /acc/ landing page** — single indexable URL describing ACC-funded treatment, the rate, no referral required, claim process. Addresses G7.
- **#31 (NEW) — Rewrite condition + service intros with a citation-ready 40–60 word direct answer first.** Addresses G24.

### Tier 3 (3 items)
- **#14 — blog, 2 posts/month** (per-condition evidence-based posts with Physician schema, datePublished, FAQs). Addresses G8.
- **#15 — Reddit / community seeding** (90% non-promotional; r/newzealand / r/chch / r/AskNZ). Addresses G15.
- **#16 — monthly review nudge via Resend.**

## Notes for next run (2026-05-22)

1. **Indexing-lag calibration.** #30 (cross-links from osteopathy intro + homepage) shipped 2026-05-10. By next Saturday, Google has had ~12 days to re-index. If `/conditions/sciatica/`, `/conditions/headaches-migraines/`, `/conditions/pregnancy-back-pain/`, `/conditions/sports-injury-recovery/`, `/conditions/neck-pain/`, `/conditions/back-pain/` are still invisible for their target queries, the bottleneck is content quality / freshness (items #14 + #31), not internal-link surface — at that point #14 and #31 become higher-priority than they currently look.
2. **Suburb-signal calibration.** #29 added Hillmorton / Wigram / Addington to flowing intro copy 2026-05-10. Next run's `osteopath hillmorton`, `osteopath wigram`, `osteopath addington` SERPs are the leading indicators on whether Google picks up the signal at all. If still all-UK after re-indexing, the in-content sentence alone isn't enough and the longer play (small /find-us/ page) becomes worth considering.
3. **First-run check on `whiplash osteopath christchurch` and `concussion osteopath christchurch`** — both added this week.
4. **Cranial osteopath christchurch** is at priority 20. If it stays ≤ 20 after the next run, it's the first prune candidate.
5. **Autocomplete still blocked.** The durable fix is widening the routine egress allowlist to include `suggestqueries.google.com` and `meridianosteopathy.co.nz`. Without it, the pool can only expand via SERP-derived queries, which is a weaker signal than what users actually type.
